figures.caption: Figure 8. Ablation study on GCN depth. We evaluate the performance impact of varying GCN layers (from 1 to 4) across five benchmarks. The results demonstrate that the 2-layer architecture consistently yields optimal performance across different LLM backbones.

Table 1. Comparison of task performance and P90 inference latency between baseline methods and GraphFlow. Experiments are conducted using Qwen-2.5-7B, Llama-3.1-8B, and Gemma-2-9B across five benchmarks spanning mathematical reasoning, complex question answering, and code generation.
Figure 2. The overall framework of our proposed GraphFlow.

Figure 9. Additional visualizations of residual KV sparsity. We show the element-wise difference heatmaps for keys (∆K) and values (∆V ) at representative layers and attention heads, comparing contextualized KV states against the context-free base cache.
Figure 5. Memory-performance trade-off analysis. Comparison of memory footprint and task performance.
Figure 6. Memory consumption with increasing batch sizes.
Figure 3. Visualization of KV sparsity. We evaluate the elementwise difference matrices (∆K,∆V ) between stateful and stateless base KV caches.
Table 2. Component ablation results for task-adaptive workflow generation using the Llama-3.1-8B backbone.
